verses.1: “But now those who are younger than I have me in derision,
verses.2: Of what use is the strength of their hands to me,
verses.3: They are gaunt from lack and famine.
verses.4: They pluck salt herbs by the bushes.
verses.5: They are driven out from among men.
verses.6: so that they live in frightful valleys,
verses.8: They are children of fools, yes, children of wicked men.
verses.10: They abhor me, they stand aloof from me,
verses.11: For he has untied his cord, and afflicted me;
verses.12: On my right hand rise the rabble.
verses.14: As through a wide breach they come.
verses.16: “Now my soul is poured out within me.
verses.17: In the night season my bones are pierced in me,
verses.18: My garment is disfigured by great force.
verses.19: He has cast me into the mire.
verses.20: I cry to you, and you do not answer me.
verses.21: You have turned to be cruel to me.
verses.22: You lift me up to the wind, and drive me with it.
verses.23: For I know that you will bring me to death,
verses.24: “However doesn’t one stretch out a hand in his fall?
verses.25: Didn’t I weep for him who was in trouble?
verses.26: When I looked for good, then evil came.
verses.27: My heart is troubled, and doesn’t rest.
verses.28: I go mourning without the sun.
verses.29: I am a brother to jackals,
verses.30: My skin grows black and peels from me.
verses.31: Therefore my harp has turned to mourning,
